raw_text:
Velocity Labs

Building intuitive interfaces that scale

MANAGER, FRONTEND ENGINEERING

San Francisco, CA (Remote-first, US-based) · Full-time · $210,000 - $270,000 USD annually

Velocity Labs is seeking an experienced Manager of Frontend Engineering to lead and grow our

frontend team. You'll combine hands-on technical expertise with people management, mentoring 4-6

frontend engineers while contributing to the technical direction of our platform. This is an ideal role for a

senior engineer ready to step into leadership, balancing strategic planning with direct involvement in

critical frontend initiatives.

ABOUT US

Velocity Labs is a fast-growing SaaS company focused on providing real-time analytics and data
visualization solutions for enterprise clients. We believe great user experiences are built on solid

engineering foundations, cutting-edge technology, and collaborative teams. Our platform serves
thousands of users globally, and our frontend teams are central to delivering compelling, performant

interfaces.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Lead, mentor, and develop a team of 4-6 frontend engineers, fostering a collaborative culture
focused on excellence, continuous learning, and technical growth

Conduct regular one-on-ones, performance reviews, and career development planning to support
team member growth and retention

Drive the technical roadmap for frontend initiatives, collaborating with product and design teams to
translate requirements into scalable React/Next.js architecture

Champion best practices in code quality, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1), and web
performance optimization across the team

Actively participate in critical features and complex refactoring efforts—staying hands-on with
React, TypeScript, and modern tooling (Vite, Webpack)

Identify and resolve technical debt, establishing standards for component architecture, testing
strategies, and CSS-in-JS/Tailwind patterns

Conduct code reviews and technical interviews, maintaining high standards for code quality and

mentoring junior engineers

Advocate for frontend engineering needs in company planning and resource allocation meetings

REQUIRED

 6+ years of professional frontend development experience, with 2+ years in a people

management or technical leadership role

Expert-level proficiency in React and TypeScript, with demonstrable experience building large-

scale applications

Strong understanding of modern frontend tooling (Vite, Webpack, build optimization) and bundling

strategies

Proven experience mentoring and growing engineering teams; experience with hiring, onboarding,

and performance management

Deep knowledge of web performance metrics (Core Web Vitals, Lighthouse) and optimization

techniques

Excellent communication skills and ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders (product,

design, backend)

NICE TO HAVE

Experience with Next.js and server-side rendering patterns (SSR, SSG, ISR)

Expertise in web accessibility (WCAG 2.1 standards) and inclusive design practices

Familiarity with design systems and component library development

Experience with monitoring and observability tools for frontend performance tracking

TECH STACK

React

  TypeScript

  Next.js   Tailwind CSS   Vite   Webpack   Web Accessibility (WCAG)

Web Performance Optimization   CSS-in-JS (Styled Components / Emotion)

  Testing frameworks (Jest, React Testing Library)

BENEFITS

Competitive equity package with meaningful ownership stakes

Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage

Unlimited PTO and flexible work schedule with remote-first culture

Professional development budget ($3,000/year) for conferences, courses, and certifications

Parental leave (16 weeks primary, 8 weeks secondary)

Free lunch program and home office setup stipend

Quarterly team offsites and annual company retreat

HOW TO APPLY

Submit your resume, a cover letter highlighting your leadership experience, and a link to your GitHub
profile or portfolio showcasing recent React/TypeScript work to careers@velocitylabs.io. Applicants will
first go through a brief phone screen with our Head of Engineering, followed by a technical discussion
and team culture conversation with the leadership team.

Expertise in web accessibility (WCAG 2.1 standards) and inclusive design practices
Familiarity with design systems and component library development
Experience with monitoring and observability tools for frontend performance tracking
